content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree TTT consisting of NNN nodes, numbered 111 to NNN, and the tree is rooted at node 111. The parent of the iii-th node is PiP_iPi​ (111 has no parent because it is the root).

Define subtree(x)subtree(x)subtree(x) as the set of nodes yyy such that the unique path from node 111 to node yyy passes through xxx. Note that this means x∈subtree(x)x \in subtree(x)x∈subtree(x). A vertex xxx is called a **leaf** if ∣subtree(x)∣=1|subtree(x)| = 1∣subtree(x)∣=1, i.e. it's subtree consists of only itself.

A array AAA of size NNN is said to be valid when the following conditions hold:



For all nodes XXX which are not leaves, AX=MEX†({AY,Y∈subtree(x)A_X = MEX^{\dagger}(\{A_Y, Y \in subtree(x)AX​=MEX†({AY​,Y∈subtree(x) and Y≠X})Y \ne X \})Y=X}).

Less formally, you require AXA_XAX​ to be the MEX of all elements in it's subtree (excluding itself). For leaves, there are no constraints on AXA_XAX​.




AAA is a permutation of the integers in [0,N−1][0, N - 1][0,N−1].



Count the number of possible arrays AAA modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

†^{\dagger}† MEX(S)MEX(S)MEX(S) is defined as the smallest non-negative integer not present in SSS.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the tree.
The second line contains N−1N - 1N−1 integers - P2,P3,…,PNP_2, P_3, \ldots, P_NP2​,P3​,…,PN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of possible arrays AAA modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 2.5⋅1052.5 \cdot 10^52.5⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The 222 valid arrays are [0,1][0, 1][0,1] and [1,0][1, 0][1,0]. In both cases, A1=MEX({A2})A_1 = MEX(\{A_2\})A1​=MEX({A2​}).
Test Case 2 : All 666 permutations of [0,1,2][0, 1, 2][0,1,2] are valid.
Test Case 3 : The valid arrays are [2,0,1][2, 0, 1][2,0,1], [2,1,0][2, 1, 0][2,1,0], [1,0,2][1, 0, 2][1,0,2].
